publications.headline: Widespread Supply Chain Attacks, Critical Zero-Days in Linux & cPanel, and Soaring AI-Driven Ransomware Mark a Turbulent Week

articles.headline: Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Portkey, an AI Gateway Startup, to Secure Autonomous AI Agents
articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks has announced its planned acquisition of Portkey, a startup specializing in AI Gateway technology. The move is a strategic effort to address the emerging security challenges posed by the rise of autonomous AI agents in the enterprise. Portkey provides a centralized control plane to manage, monitor, and secure AI agent traffic. Its technology will be integrated into Palo Alto Networks' Prisma AIRS platform, creating a 'central nervous system' to inspect AI transactions, enforce governance, and apply least-privilege controls. This acquisition reflects a growing industry focus on securing the new, unmanaged attack surface created by highly privileged AI agents.

articles.headline: UK Cyber Survey: 43% of Businesses Breached, But Only 25% Have an IR Plan
articles.summary: The UK government's latest Cyber Security Breaches Survey for 2025/2026 reveals a landscape of persistent risk, with 43% of businesses (an estimated 612,000) experiencing a breach in the past year. Phishing remains the dominant attack vector, implicated in 85% of successful breaches. Despite the high frequency of attacks, the report exposes critical gaps in organizational readiness: only 25% of businesses have a formal incident response plan. The survey also highlights major weaknesses in supply chain risk management and a lack of security practices for adopting AI, prompting criticism that current UK policy is insufficient to address national cyber risk.

articles.headline: ADT Security Hit by Data Breach; ShinyHunters Claims 5.5 Million Records Stolen
articles.summary: Home security giant ADT has confirmed a data breach that exposed the personal information of 5.5 million unique individuals. The incident was initiated by a voice phishing (vishing) attack on an employee, which allowed the extortion group ShinyHunters to gain access to the employee's Okta single sign-on account. The attackers then pivoted to ADT's Salesforce instance to exfiltrate customer data. While ShinyHunters claimed to have stolen 10 million records, analysis by Have I Been Pwned confirmed 5.5 million unique email addresses. ADT states the compromised data was 'limited' to names, addresses, and phone numbers, and that no financial or security system data was accessed.

articles.headline: AI-Enabled Cybercrime Fuels 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims, Fortinet Reports
articles.summary: The 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report from Fortinet's FortiGuard Labs reveals a dramatic industrialization of cybercrime, heavily augmented by artificial intelligence. The report highlights a staggering 389% year-over-year increase in confirmed ransomware victims, a surge attributed to the proliferation of AI-powered crime-as-a-service tools like WormGPT and FraudGPT. Furthermore, AI is enabling attackers to move with unprecedented speed, shrinking the average time-to-exploit for critical vulnerabilities to just 24-48 hours. This acceleration leaves defenders with a dangerously small window to respond and underscores a fundamental shift in the threat landscape towards faster, more sophisticated, and scalable attacks.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity Pros Jailed for 4 Years for Aiding BlackCat Ransomware Gang
articles.summary: In a significant case of insiders turning to crime, two American cybersecurity professionals, Ryan Goldberg and Kevin Martin, have each been sentenced to four years in prison. They pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges for acting as affiliates of the notorious BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware gang. Between April and December 2023, the defendants used their expert knowledge to conduct ransomware attacks against U.S. organizations, including a healthcare provider. They extorted victims, laundered payments, and in one case, leaked sensitive patient data. The case highlights the dangerous threat of malicious insiders and the abuse of specialized skills for criminal profit within the ransomware-as-a-service ecosystem.

articles.headline: QR Code Phishing Surges 146% in Q1 2026, Microsoft Warns
articles.summary: According to Microsoft's Q1 2026 threat intelligence report, QR code-based phishing (quishing) has become the fastest-growing email attack vector, with volumes jumping 146% between January and March. Attackers are increasingly hiding malicious QR codes within PDF attachments to bypass email security scanners. These campaigns, often using CAPTCHA-gated landing pages to further evade detection, aim to steal user credentials. In Q1, Microsoft detected 8.3 billion email phishing threats in total, with one major campaign in March targeting 179,000 organizations across 43 countries, highlighting the scale and sophistication of modern Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) operations like Tycoon2FA.

articles.headline: Ransomware Civil War: KryBit RaaS Hacks and Leaks Rival Gang 0APT
articles.summary: In an unusual turn of events within the cybercrime underworld, the new KryBit ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group has hacked and publicly leaked the entire operational infrastructure of a rival gang, 0APT. The leak appears to be retaliation for 0APT previously leaking some of KryBit's data. The exposed data revealed that 0APT had been fabricating victim claims and was running its leak site from a shockingly amateur setup: an Android device. While 0APT is left exposed, KryBit continues its own attacks, employing double extortion tactics against at least 20 victims across multiple sectors and countries, including the US, Germany, and Japan.

articles.headline: That AI Extension Helping You Write? It's Actually a RAT Stealing Your Data
articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has discovered at least 18 malicious browser extensions masquerading as Generative AI productivity tools. These extensions, some downloaded by thousands, are not enhancing productivity but are instead functioning as Remote Access Trojans (RATs), infostealers, and search hijackers. The attackers leverage the broad permissions users grant to these 'AI assistants' to intercept sensitive data, including credentials, proprietary code shared in AI prompts, and private communications. The research provides technical case studies, including an extension that establishes a persistent command-and-control channel to execute arbitrary code within a user's authenticated sessions, highlighting a severe risk to both individuals and enterprises.
malware: Chrome MCP Server - AI Browser Control

articles.headline: CISA Mandates Patch for Actively Exploited Windows NTLM Hash Leak Flaw
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a Windows information disclosure vulnerability, CVE-2026-32202, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog due to evidence of active exploitation. The zero-click flaw allows an attacker to steal NTLM hashes (hashed user passwords) from a target machine, which can then be used in pass-the-hash attacks for lateral movement. The vulnerability exists due to an incomplete patch for a previous flaw. CISA has issued a directive requiring all Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the necessary patches by May 12, 2026, highlighting the significant risk it poses.

articles.headline: Copy.Fail: New Linux Kernel Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Allows Instant Root Access
articles.summary: A critical high-severity vulnerability, dubbed "Copy.Fail" and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been discovered in the Linux kernel, affecting nearly all major distributions released since 2017. The flaw, rated with a CVSS score of 7.8, is a logic bug in the kernel's crypto API that allows an unprivileged local user to perform a controlled memory write. A publicly available proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit demonstrates how this can be used to modify a setuid binary like `/usr/bin/su` in memory, achieving full root access reliably and within seconds. The exploit is particularly dangerous in multi-tenant environments like Kubernetes and CI/CD systems, as it leaves no trace on disk, evading simple detection methods.

articles.headline: MOVEit Automation Hit with Critical 9.8 CVSS Auth Bypass Flaw (CVE-2026-4670)
articles.summary: Progress Software has issued an urgent security alert for a critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-4670, in its MOVEit Automation software. The flaw, which has been assigned a CVSS score of 9.8, could allow an unauthenticated attacker to gain unauthorized access and potentially administrative control over the system. Given the history of mass exploitation targeting the MOVEit product suite, this vulnerability poses a significant risk. Progress has released patched versions and strongly urges all customers to upgrade immediately, noting that the update requires a full installer and will cause a system outage.

articles.headline: Mini Shai-Hulud: Massive Supply Chain Attack Compromises SAP, PyTorch, and Intercom Packages
articles.summary: A sophisticated supply chain attack campaign named "Mini Shai-Hulud," attributed to the threat actor group TeamPCP, has compromised popular open-source packages from SAP, PyTorch Lightning, and Intercom on both NPM and PyPI repositories. The attack, which began on April 29, 2026, involves injecting malicious preinstall scripts into widely used developer packages, collectively downloaded millions of times. The malware is designed to steal a vast array of credentials, including local secrets, GitHub tokens, and cloud service keys (AWS, Azure, GCP), exfiltrating them to attacker-controlled infrastructure. The campaign also features a worm-like propagation mechanism, using stolen GitHub tokens to inject malicious code into other repositories, significantly amplifying its reach and impacting an estimated 1,800 developers so far.

articles.headline: cPanel Zero-Day Auth Bypass (CVE-2026-41940) Actively Exploited for Months Before Patch
articles.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-41940, in the widely used cPanel & WHM web hosting platform was actively exploited as a zero-day for at least two months before a patch was released. The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 9.8, allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to gain full administrative access to a vulnerable server. The vulnerability is a CRLF injection weakness that allows an attacker to manipulate session files and create an administrator-level session without credentials. Hosting providers observed exploitation as early as February 2026, long before the official advisory on April 28. With an estimated 1.5 million cPanel instances exposed online, administrators are urged to patch immediately.

publications.headline: Critical Flaws in Linux and Windows Exploited, GitHub RCE, and Major Data Breaches at Medtronic & Udemy

articles.headline: CISA and Partners Release Joint Guidance for Applying Zero Trust Principles to OT Environments
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), in partnership with the Department of War, DOE, FBI, and DOS, has published a comprehensive guide for implementing Zero Trust security principles within Operational Technology (OT) environments. The guidance provides a roadmap for securing critical infrastructure, addressing the unique challenges of OT such as legacy systems, uptime requirements, and the paramount need for physical process safety, especially in the face of threats like Volt Typhoon.

articles.headline: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave
articles.summary: Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) report warns of an industrialized cybercrime landscape where AI, encryption, and proxies are making attacks faster and more sophisticated. The report highlights a significant shift in ransomware tactics towards data theft and extortion, moving away from pure encryption. It also notes a concerning trend of state-sponsored actors using cybercriminal groups as proxies, blurring the lines between espionage and financially motivated crime.

articles.headline: Fortinet Report: AI-Enabled Attacks Fuel 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims
articles.summary: Fortinet's 2026 Global Threat Landscape Report reveals a staggering 389% year-over-year increase in confirmed ransomware victims in 2025, attributing the surge in part to AI-enabled cybercrime. The report highlights how tools like WormGPT and FraudGPT are making attacks more sophisticated and scalable. A key finding is the dramatic reduction in the time-to-exploit for critical vulnerabilities, which has shrunk to between 24 and 48 hours, demanding a more agile and industrialized defense.

articles.headline: HITRUST Analysis Confirms Significant Rise in AI-Enabled Cyberattacks in Q1 2026
articles.summary: HITRUST's Q1 2026 Cyber Threat Adaptive (CTA) analysis confirms a significant and continued increase in AI-enabled cyberattacks. The report details how adversaries are leveraging AI to automate deception (e.g., deepfakes, advanced phishing), discover complex vulnerabilities, and exploit AI-enabled systems directly. This trend is compressing the time between vulnerability discovery and exploitation, highlighting the need for dynamic, threat-adaptive security frameworks like the HITRUST CSF to keep pace with the evolving threat landscape.

articles.headline: 15-Year-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Stealthy Root Access via Crafted Certificate
articles.summary: A high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-35414 or 'SplitSSHell', has been discovered in OpenSSH versions released over the past 15 years. With a CVSS score of 8.1, the flaw allows an authenticated attacker with a valid certificate to use a specially crafted principal name to bypass access controls and gain full root shell access. A major concern is that the attack is stealthy, leaving no trace in standard system logs, which makes detection extremely difficult. All organizations are urged to update to OpenSSH 10.3 or newer.

articles.headline: Critical Flaw in VECT 2.0 Ransomware Turns It Into a Destructive Wiper, Permanently Destroying Large Files
articles.summary: Check Point Research has discovered a critical implementation flaw in the VECT 2.0 ransomware that causes it to permanently destroy any file larger than 128KB, effectively turning it into a data wiper. The bug, found in all variants targeting Windows, Linux, and ESXi, relates to improper handling of cryptographic nonces. This means that even if a victim pays the ransom, recovery of most valuable enterprise data is impossible, as the keys required for decryption are discarded during the encryption process.

articles.headline: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims Theft of 9 Million Records and Corporate Data
articles.summary: Medical device manufacturer Medtronic has confirmed it sustained a cybersecurity incident following claims by the ShinyHunters threat group. The group alleged it stole over 9 million records and terabytes of corporate data, issuing a 'pay or leak' ultimatum. Medtronic stated the breach was contained to its corporate IT systems and, due to network segmentation, had no impact on medical products, patient safety, or manufacturing operations. An investigation into the scope of the data access is ongoing.

articles.headline: New Stealthy Python Backdoor 'DEEP#DOOR' Steals Credentials Using Legitimate Tunneling Service
articles.summary: Security researchers from Securonix have discovered a sophisticated, multi-stage Python backdoor named DEEP#DOOR. The malware operates as a full-featured Remote Access Trojan (RAT), beginning with an obfuscated batch script that embeds the Python payload. It disables Windows security controls, establishes persistence through multiple methods (Startup folder, Run keys, Scheduled Tasks, WMI), and uses the legitimate 'bore.pub' tunneling service for stealthy command-and-control and data exfiltration, focusing on browser and cloud credentials.

articles.headline: ShinyHunters Leaks 1.4 Million Udemy User Records, Including Financial Data, After Failed Extortion
articles.summary: The ShinyHunters cybercrime group has publicly leaked a database containing 1.4 million records from the online learning platform Udemy after a 'pay or leak' extortion attempt failed. The compromised data is extensive, including user and instructor PII, contact information, and sensitive financial payout details like PayPal accounts and bank information. The breach places affected individuals at a high risk of phishing, fraud, and identity theft. Users are advised to reset passwords and enable 2FA.

articles.headline: Actively Exploited Windows Zero-Day (CVE-2026-32202) Steals NTLM Hashes Without User Clicks
articles.summary: Microsoft and CISA are warning of active, zero-click exploitation of a Windows Shell spoofing vulnerability, CVE-2026-32202. The flaw, which stems from an incomplete patch for a bug previously used by APT28, allows attackers to steal Net-NTLMv2 hashes simply by having a user open a folder containing a malicious file. This enables NTLM relay and pass-the-hash attacks. CISA has added the vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, mandating that U.S. federal agencies apply the patch immediately.

articles.headline: Critical 'Copy Fail' Linux Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Gives Instant Root on Major Distros
articles.summary: A critical logic flaw in the Linux kernel, dubbed 'Copy Fail' and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been disclosed, affecting nearly all major distributions since 2017. Discovered by Theori, the vulnerability allows any unprivileged local user to gain full root privileges with a simple, highly reliable exploit that does not depend on race conditions. This poses a severe risk to multi-tenant environments like cloud hosting, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines. A public proof-of-concept exists, and major Linux vendors are rushing to release patches.

articles.headline: Critical GitHub RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-3854) Allowed Full Server Compromise via Single 'git push'
articles.summary: A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in GitHub, tracked as CVE-2026-3854, has been disclosed by security firm Wiz. The flaw allowed any authenticated user with push access to a repository to execute arbitrary commands on GitHub's backend servers using a specially crafted `git push` command. The bug affected both GitHub.com and self-hosted GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) instances, potentially leading to the compromise of millions of repositories or full server takeovers. Patches are available, but a high percentage of internet-facing GHES instances remain vulnerable.

updated.originalHeadline: Ransomware Negotiator Admits to Conspiring with BlackCat Gang
updated.updateTitle: Update: Ransomware Negotiator Admits to Conspiring with BlackCat Gang
updated.updateSummary: Two cybersecurity professionals, Ryan Goldberg and Kevin Martin, sentenced to 4 years for acting as BlackCat affiliates, conducting attacks and leaking patient data.

updated.originalHeadline: TeamPCP Weaponizes npm with Malicious Bitwarden CLI in Sophisticated Supply Chain Attack
updated.updateTitle: Update: TeamPCP Weaponizes npm with Malicious Bitwarden CLI in Sophisticated Supply Chain Attack
updated.updateSummary: TeamPCP's 'Mini Shai-Hulud' campaign expands, compromising SAP, PyTorch, and Intercom packages on NPM/PyPI, impacting 1,800+ developers with worm-like credential theft.

updated.originalHeadline: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data
updated.updateTitle: Update: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data
updated.updateSummary: KryBit continues active ransomware operations with new TTPs, IOCs, and 20+ victims, despite its feud with 0APT.

updated.originalHeadline: Actively Exploited Windows Zero-Day (CVE-2026-32202) Steals NTLM Hashes Without User Clicks
updated.updateTitle: Update: Actively Exploited Windows Zero-Day (CVE-2026-32202) Steals NTLM Hashes Without User Clicks
updated.updateSummary: CISA issues binding directive for FCEB agencies to patch CVE-2026-32202 by May 12, 2026.

updated.originalHeadline: Fortinet Report: AI-Enabled Attacks Fuel 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims
updated.updateTitle: Update: Fortinet Report: AI-Enabled Attacks Fuel 389% Surge in Ransomware Victims
updated.updateSummary: New details from Fortinet's report explicitly map AI's role in cyberattacks to MITRE ATT&CK tactics and techniques, and refine targeted sectors.

updated.originalHeadline: Patching Windows Collapse as Time-to-Exploit for Vulnerabilities Shrinks Dramatically
updated.updateTitle: Update: Patching Windows Collapse as Time-to-Exploit for Vulnerabilities Shrinks Dramatically
updated.updateSummary: Fortinet reports 389% surge in ransomware victims and time-to-exploit shrinking to 24-48 hours, driven by AI-enabled attacks.

updated.originalHeadline: Unit 42: Frontier AI Models Can Autonomously Find Zero-Days, Posing Major Threat to Software Security
updated.updateTitle: Update: Unit 42: Frontier AI Models Can Autonomously Find Zero-Days, Posing Major Threat to Software Security
updated.updateSummary: HITRUST confirms significant rise in AI-enabled cyberattacks in Q1 2026, detailing new methods like deepfakes and exploiting AI systems.

updated.originalHeadline: Decade-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Full Root Access, Exploits Hard to Detect
updated.updateTitle: Update: Decade-Old OpenSSH Flaw (CVE-2026-35414) Allows Full Root Access, Exploits Hard to Detect
updated.updateSummary: The 15-year-old OpenSSH flaw, CVE-2026-35414, has been dubbed 'SplitSSHell', with new reports reiterating its critical impact.

updated.originalHeadline: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Windows Flaw to KEV Catalog After Botched Patch for APT28 Zero-Day
updated.updateTitle: Update: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Windows Flaw to KEV Catalog After Botched Patch for APT28 Zero-Day
updated.updateSummary: Microsoft and CISA confirm active exploitation of CVE-2026-32202, detailing Windows Shell spoofing and NTLM relay attacks, and adding SMB signing as a key mitigation.
